facade_colour: warm red brick with buff-yellow brick bands and arches, plus pale sage-green doors, garage doors, eaves, and verge trim
roof_type: Steep gabled roofline with repeated front-facing rear gables

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red brick with buff-yellow brick bands and arches, plus pale sage-green doors, garage doors, eaves, and verge trim
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: The row appears maintained with intact masonry and recently painted trim, though there is minor weathering and patchy winter staining at the lower steps and paving.
photo_observations.window_details: Each house has vertically proportioned rear windows with replacement glazing, buff-brick segmental arches, and narrow attic windows set into steep front-facing rear gables.
photo_observations.door_details: Individual rear entries sit above concrete stoops with black metal railings; several units also have pale green overhead garage doors at grade.
photo_observations.ground_floor_arches: segmental brick arches over selected rear openings
photo_observations.cornice_details: Painted wood bargeboards and fascia highlight the repeated Victorian rear gables rather than a projecting cornice.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: No bay windows visible from the rear lane view.
photo_observations.porch_details: Raised concrete stoops serve the rear entries, with short runs of steps, black railings, and retaining walls.
photo_observations.chimney_details: A few brick chimneys and vent stacks are visible above the shared roofline toward the middle and far end of the row.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Repeated buff-brick arches, string courses, diaper brick ventilation panels, and carved wooden gable trim give the row a strong late-Victorian identity.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Steep gabled roofline with repeated front-facing rear gables
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Replacement windows, rear garage doors, and minor utility additions are visible, but the repeated masonry and gable form remain legible.
photo_observations.notes: Wide rear-lane group view of the Oxford Street rowhouses, showing the repeated gables and rear access arrangement clearly.

notes: Wide rear-lane group view of the Oxford Street rowhouses, showing the repeated gables and rear access arrangement clearly.
ground_floor_arch_type: segmental brick arches over selected rear openings

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a steep gabled roofline with repeated front-facing rear gables, consistent with its house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 10, 10, 5 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 5 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include string courses, cornice.

facade_description: 30A-32E Oxford St presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a steep gabled roofline with repeated front-facing rear gables, consistent with its house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 10, 10, 5 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 5 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include string courses, cornice.
